game_name: Glory of Heracles IV: Gift from the Gods

danger_signals.meaning: Core danger state—player character exists in vulnerable supernatural condition

danger_signals.visual_form: Sunken or corrupted architecture, underwater/ruined environments
danger_signals.meaning: World is in a state of mythological catastrophe requiring intervention

danger_signals.visual_form: Unleashed chaos, corrupted creatures, scattered divine artifacts
danger_signals.meaning: Widespread danger from unleashed supernatural forces

safety_signals.visual_form: Presence of gods or celestial imagery
safety_signals.meaning: Player can seek aid from Olympian deities

safety_signals.visual_form: Character invulnerability visual effects (predecessor game mechanic)
safety_signals.meaning: Protection from physical harm in specific circumstances

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Hades realm, dark caverns, underworld locations
Sunken Atlantis, corrupted environments, scattered divine artifacts

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Protagonist depicted as spectral or ethereal entity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Character movement and interaction mechanics restricted to non-corporeal state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Underwater ruins, sunken architecture, environmental decay
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Navigational challenges in submerged or destroyed locations

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Scattered chaotic elements, corrupted creature designs, magical disturbances
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemies display unnatural behaviors reflecting unleashed forces

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Celestial light effects, divine presence indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs offer aid or prophecy; gods become accessible allies

environmental_storytelling.example: Atlantis depicted as sunken, ruined civilization—visual reminder of catastrophe and lost glory
Once-divine or civilized spaces rendered broken/twisted—indicates corruption spread by Pandora's forces
Olympus portrayed as celestial/heavenly vs. Hades as dark/infernal—clear visual demarcation of divine allegiances
Protagonist's spirit separation visually communicates the central crisis driving the entire narrative

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Quest to reunite spirit with body; dependent on divine aid
Offer aid to protagonist; represent order and divine will
Exhibit unnatural aggression reflecting Pandora's unleashed forces
Static environmental hazards and ruins; no active civilization

notes: Glory of Heracles IV operates within a Greek mythology paradigm where world-state is communicated through narrative catastrophes (Atlantis sinking, Pandora's Box unleashed, spirit-body separation) rather than through faction color-coding or traditional RPG political systems. The game's danger and safety signals are primarily supernatural in nature. Limited visual/mechanical documentation available for T3 tier; analysis based on narrative framework and predecessor game mechanics (immortality system, party-based structure, divine assistance mechanics). The 'other' paradigm classification reflects this game's emphasis on mythological narrative and supernatural states rather than conventional fantasy RPG faction conflicts.
sources_used: Wikipedia: Glory of Heracles (series overview and game descriptions)
